help.advanced.adaptiveAi.content: The Adaptive IA engine detects your hardware at runtime — WebGPU, WebNN, DirectML, CPU cores, VRAM tier, and battery — and routes local inference to the fastest available backend. Enable it under Configurações → Early Access Features and inspect the live readout under Configurações → IA. Eco Mode picks the smallest viable model to save battery and memory, and a tab-leader election ensures only one browser tab uses the GPU at a time to avoid VRAM collisions.
help.advanced.adaptiveAi.title: Adaptive IA, GPU & Eco Mode
help.advanced.cloudSync.content: Optional end-to-end encrypted sync keeps your library in step across devices using a Cloudflare R2 backend. Enable “Cloud sync” under Configurações → Early Access Features and configure it under Configurações → Connections. Project data is encrypted locally with AES-256-GCM before upload, and your IA API keys are never sent to the cloud. Sync is entirely opt-in; with it off, StoryCraft remains a fully offline-first app.
help.advanced.cloudSync.title: Sincronização na nuvem
help.advanced.encryption.content: Protect every manuscript, snapshot, and setting stored on your device with AES-256-GCM encryption derived from a passphrase (PBKDF2, 600,000 iterations). Enable it under Configurações → Privacy & Security → “Encrypt project data at rest”. On the next launch an unlock dialog asks for your passphrase; you can change or disable it later from the same panel. Your passphrase never leaves the device and cannot be recovered — export an encrypted library backup before you experiment.
help.advanced.encryption.title: Criptografia em repouso
help.advanced.languages.content: StoryCraft Studio ships in seven interface languages — German, English, French, Spanish, Italian, plus Arabic and Hebrew as a right-to-left (RTL) Beta. Switch language in Configurações → General, the Bem-vindo Portal, or the Command Palette. Selecting Arabic or Hebrew flips the whole interface to RTL and loads self-hosted Noto Sans Arabic/Hebrew fonts (with Noto Naskh Arabic for the manuscript editor) for crisp glyphs. Your manuscript text always follows its own script direction, so you can mix Latin and RTL passages freely. RTL translations are a community-improvable Beta; help articles currently stay in English.
help.advanced.languages.title: Idiomas e da direita para a esquerda (RTL)
help.advanced.lora.content: LoRA adapters let a local model learn your personal writing voice without sending a single word to the cloud. Enable “LoRA adapter inference” under Configurações → Early Access Features, then open Configurações → IA → Fine-Tuning to load a pre-trained .safetensors adapter for a compatible WebLLM or Ollama base model. The dedicated LoRA view walks you through dataset extraction, training presets (Writer Style Light → Full Style Clone), and a side-by-side style evaluation. Training runs entirely on your device via a Python sidecar; the adapter influence is tunable with the scale (α) control.
help.advanced.lora.title: Ajuste fino do estilo LoRA
help.advanced.plugins.content: Extend the editor with sandboxed plugins that can only touch the capabilities they declare. Enable “Plugin system” under Configurações → Early Access Features, then manage installed plugins under Configurações → Plugins. Each plugin runs behind a permission-gated API with no direct access to the Redux store, network, or file system beyond what it requests. Reference plugins — a word-count overlay and a scene appender — show the descriptor format.
help.advanced.plugins.title: Plug-ins
help.advanced.proforge.content: ProForge is an eight-stage agentic editor that reads your whole manuscript and walks you from structural notes down to copy-edit and proofing. Enable it under Configurações → Early Access Features, then launch it from the IA tools panel in the Writer view. Every stage pauses for your review — the manuscript is never modified without explicit approval, and every change is reversible. A heuristic Supervisor flags low-confidence or fallback results so you can retry a stage with a sharper prompt.
help.advanced.proforge.title: Pipeline de edição do ProForge
help.advanced.voice.content: Drive StoryCraft Studio hands-free: dictate prose, run commands, and hear IA responses read aloud. Turn on “Voice commands & dictation” under Configurações → Early Access Features, then configure activation (manual button, push-to-talk, or a wake word) under Configurações → Voice & Speech. The default engine uses your browser’s built-in speech with zero downloads; an optional offline Whisper.cpp WASM engine keeps audio fully on-device. All processing stays local unless you explicitly enable a cloud fallback.
help.advanced.voice.title: Controle de voz e ditado
help.ai.description: Can't find an answer? Ask our IA assistant a question about any feature.
help.ai.error: Sorry, I could not reach the IA assistant. Check your provider settings and try again.
help.ai.initialMessage: Hello! I'm your IA guide for StoryCraft Studio. How can I help you today? You can ask me anything, or start with one of the suggestions below.
help.ai.title: IA Ajuda Assistant
help.aiStudio.critic.content:
IA Critic

The Critic tool reads a selected manuscript passage and returns structured feedback on three dimensions: pacing, clarity, and tone. It is not a grammar checker — it evaluates narrative craft.

  • How to use: In the IA Writing Studio, select the Critic tab. Highlight one or more manuscript paragraphs in the editor, then click Analyze. The scratchpad shows a rated report with one actionable suggestion per dimension.
  • Pacing: Flags sections that feel too rushed (short sentences, rapid scene cuts) or too slow (long expository blocks with low conflict).
  • Clarity: Highlights ambiguous pronoun references, overly nested clauses, or vocabulary mismatches with the surrounding prose style.
  • Tone: Detects tone drift — e.g. a comedic aside embedded in a high-tension scene — and suggests whether it is intentional or accidental.
  • RAG context: When RAG context is enabled, the Critic receives additional manuscript chunks so it can compare the selected passage against the rest of your story, catching consistency issues that single-passage analysis would miss.
  • Provider: Uses the same provider, model, and creativity setting as the Writer. Switch to a higher-quality model for more nuanced feedback.
help.aiStudio.critic.title: IA Critic
help.aiStudio.localAi.content:
Local IA — WebLLM, ONNX, Transformers.js

Run IA models entirely in your browser — no API key, no internet, no data leaves your device. Enable under Configurações → IA → Local IA.

  • WebLLM (GPU): Uses WebGPU to run quantised LLMs (Llama 3.2, Phi-3.5 Mini, Gemma 2). Fast on modern GPUs; requires ~2–6 GB VRAM. Best choice for generation quality.
  • ONNX Runtime (CPU): WASM-based inference, no GPU required. Slower but works on any device. Good for short completions and classification.
  • Transformers.js: Powers the local embedding model used by the hybrid RAG index. Runs automatically; no manual configuration needed.
  • Download a model: Go to Configurações → Advanced IA → Local IA models, pick a model, and click Download. A progress bar tracks the download; large models (2–4 GB) may take several minutes on first load.
  • Switch to local: In the IA Writing Studio toolbar, change the provider to "WebLLM" or "ONNX" before generating. Inference then runs fully offline.
help.aiStudio.localAi.title: Local IA (WebLLM / ONNX)
help.aiStudio.overview.content:
Your Creative Co-Pilot

The IA Writing Studio is your dedicated space for IA-assisted writing. The workflow is designed for power and flexibility:

  1. Select Context: First, choose the manuscript section you want to work on from the dropdown menu in the 'Manuscrito Context' panel.
  2. Choose a Tool: Select one of the powerful IA tools from the top of the 'IA Tools' panel.
  3. Provide Input: Depending on the tool, you might need to select text in the editor, choose characters, or write a prompt.
  4. Gerar: Click the 'Gerar' button. The output will appear in the 'IA Scratchpad' on the right.
  5. Review and Accept: You can edit the generated text in the scratchpad. Once you're happy, use the 'Insert' or 'Replace' buttons to apply it to your manuscript.
help.aiStudio.overview.title: Overview of the IA Writing Studio
help.aiStudio.plotAi.content:
Quadro de Enredo IA: Suggest Próximo Beat

The Suggest next beat feature uses your manuscript and outline as context to propose the next scene card for the Quadro de Enredo. It is designed for moments when you know a chapter needs "something" but are unsure what.

  • Trigger: Open the Quadro de Enredo (enable Quadro de Enredo v2 in feature flags first). Click the IA ✦ button in the Quadro de Enredo toolbar to open the suggestion panel.
  • How it works: StoryCraft assembles a RAG-enriched prompt from your recent manuscript sections, existing beat cards, and your project outline. This context is sent to your configured IA provider.
  • Output: The IA returns a suggested beat title, a short description, and a recommended act placement. A preview card appears in the panel with Accept and Reject buttons.
  • Accept: Clicking Accept creates the beat card on the board in the suggested act column. You can drag it to reposition or edit the title inline.
  • Multiple suggestions: Click Suggest again to get an alternative without accepting the first. Both suggestions appear side-by-side for comparison.
  • Best results: Works best when your manuscript has at least 500 words and existing beat cards have descriptive titles. Enable RAG context in Configurações → Advanced IA for richer retrieval.
help.aiStudio.plotAi.title: Quadro de Enredo IA beats
help.aiStudio.providers.content:
IA Providers & API Keys

StoryCraft Studio connects to eight IA backends. Configure them under Configurações → IA Models and Configurações → Advanced IA. All API keys are encrypted with AES-256-GCM (PBKDF2 600 000 iterations, SHA-256) before being stored in IndexedDB — they are never transmitted to any StoryCraft server.

Cloud Providers

  • Google Gemini (default): Fast, generous free tier. Get a free API key from Google IA Studio. Recommended models: gemini-2.5-flash for everyday use, gemini-2.5-pro for complex generation tasks.
  • OpenAI: GPT-4o and GPT-4o-mini. Enter your OpenAI API key in Configurações → IA → OpenAI key. Strong at instruction-following and prose rewriting.
  • Anthropic (Claude): Claude 3.5 Sonnet and Claude 3 Haiku. Enter your API key from console.anthropic.com. Excellent for long-form narrative and nuanced tone.
  • Grok (xAI): Grok-2 via xAI API. Enter your key from the xAI developer portal. Competitive on creative tasks with a lower cost per token than GPT-4.

Local / Self-Hosted Providers

  • Ollama (local — desktop only): Runs models on your machine via http://localhost:11434. Install Ollama and run ollama pull llama3.2 to get started. The Tauri desktop app bypasses the browser's CSP restriction on localhost connections. Supports any Ollama-compatible model including fine-tuned LoRA adapters.
  • WebLLM (browser, GPU): Runs quantized LLMs in the browser via WebGPU — no API key, no internet required after first download. Supported models: Llama 3.2 1B/3B, Phi-3.5 Mini, Gemma 2 2B. Requires a WebGPU-capable GPU (~2–6 GB VRAM). Download a model under Configurações → Advanced IA → Local IA models.
  • ONNX Runtime Web (browser, CPU): WASM-based inference without a GPU. Slower than WebLLM but works on any device. Good for short completions and classification tasks.
  • Transformers.js (automatic): Powers the local embedding model used by the hybrid RAG index (MiniLM-L6-v2, 384 dimensions). Runs automatically — no configuration needed.

Hybrid Fallback Chain

Under Configurações → Advanced IA → Hybrid fallback chain, define an ordered list of providers. If the primary provider returns a rate-limit or network error, StoryCraft automatically retries with the next provider in the chain. This creates a resilient setup — for example: try Gemini first, fall back to OpenAI, fall back to Ollama on desktop.

Creativity Setting

The Creativity slider (0–1) maps to the IA temperature parameter. Use 0.2–0.4 for factual tasks (summaries, consistency checks), 0.5–0.7 for balanced prose continuation, and 0.8–1.0 for brainstorming and highly creative generation.
help.aiStudio.providers.title: IA providers & keys
help.aiStudio.ragContext.content:
Retrieval-augmented prompts (local)

When RAG context is enabled in the IA Tools panel, StoryCraft retrieves relevant manuscript chunks before calling the model. Hybrid mode blends semantic embeddings (~60%), lexical overlap (~30%), and recency (~10%).

  1. Build the index under Configurações → Advanced IA → Rebuild local search index (requires the local embedding model on capable devices).
  2. Open the IA Writing Studio, enable RAG context, and run Continuar, Brainstorm, or Critic.
  3. The chunk badge shows how many passages were injected; Quadro de Enredo beat suggestions use the same pipeline.

Your manuscript text stays in the browser; only the assembled prompt is sent to your chosen IA provider.
help.aiStudio.ragContext.title: RAG context for IA generation
help.aiStudio.tools.content:
The Right Tool for the Job

Each tool in the studio serves a different purpose:

  • Continuar Writing: Perfect for writer's block. Place your cursor in the text, and the IA will write what comes next.
  • Improve Text: Highlight a sentence or paragraph, and the IA will rewrite it. Use the Style dropdown to guide the improvement (e.g., make it more 'Cinematic').
  • Change Tone: Similar to 'Improve Text,' but uses the Tone dropdown to change the mood (e.g., make it more 'Suspenseful').
  • Gerar Dialogue: Select the characters in the scene, describe the scenario, and the IA will write their conversation.
  • Brainstorm Ideas: Get a list of creative plot points for what could happen next.
  • Summarize Section: Quickly generates a synopsis of the selected manuscript section, which is useful for checking pacing or writing chapter summaries.
help.aiStudio.tools.title: Understanding the IA Tools
help.analysis.consistency.content:
Consistency Checker

The Consistency Checker scans your manuscript and compares every character and world mention against their dossier details. It catches continuity errors automatically — like a character being described as left-handed in Chapter 1 but right-handed in Chapter 12, or a location appearing after it was destroyed on the timeline.

  • How to run: Navigate to Consistency Checker from the sidebar. Click Run full scan. The scan reads all manuscript sections and cross-references characters, worlds, and the scene timeline.
  • Warning types: Name mismatch (alias used inconsistently), Attribute conflict (a physical or personality trait contradicts the dossier), Timeline violation (a scene references an event before it occurs), Dead character mention (a character marked deceased appears alive in dialogue).
  • Reading a warning: Each warning shows the section title, the offending sentence, and a suggested fix. Click Jump to section to open the Manuscrito editor at that exact location.
  • Ignoring warnings: Click Dismiss on a warning to suppress it if the inconsistency is intentional (e.g. an unreliable narrator). Dismissed warnings are hidden until you click Show dismissed.
  • Re-scan after edits: The checker does not update in real time. After fixing an issue, click Run full scan again to clear the resolved warning from the list.
help.analysis.consistency.title: Verificador de consistência
help.analysis.crossProject.content:
Cross-Project Pesquisar

Find manuscript text, character names, and world details across every project stored in your browser — ideal for series writing or reusing world-building. Enable under Configurações → Experimental flags → Cross-project search.

  • What is indexed: Manuscrito section titles and content, character names and descriptions, world names. Images and encrypted keys are never indexed.
  • Index updates: The index rebuilds automatically on each save (debounced). A subtle background indicator appears in the status bar during indexing.
  • Privacy: The index lives entirely in your browser's IndexedDB. Nothing is sent to any server; all search runs locally.
  • How to search: Open the Command Palette (Ctrl+K) and type your query, or open the Cross-Project Pesquisar panel from the sidebar. Results show project title, section, and a short excerpt. Click a result to open that project at the matching section.
  • Reset the index: Configurações → Data → Rebuild cross-project index if results seem stale after importing a backup.
help.analysis.crossProject.title: Pesquisa entre projetos
help.analysis.health.content:
Project Health Score

A blended indicator (0–100) that gives you a quick read on how well-developed your project is. Enable under Configurações → Experimental flags → Project health score.

  • Goal completeness (25%): Whether a word-count goal and target date are set, and how much progress you have made toward them.
  • Cast depth (25%): Average number of filled fields per character profile. Characters with only a name pull the score down; fully described cast members lift it.
  • Mundo-building coverage (25%): Ratio of world and location records with descriptions and at least one linked manuscript section.
  • Manuscrito consistency (25%): Inverse of open Consistency Checker warnings — fewer warnings, higher sub-score.
  • How to improve: Expand character backstories, add world details, set a goal and target date, and resolve Consistency warnings. Each action moves the dial.
  • Location: The score ring appears on the Painel when the flag is active. Hover to see the four sub-scores individually.
help.analysis.health.title: Pontuação de integridade do projeto
help.analysis.progress.content:
Progress Tracker

The Progress Tracker view keeps you on course with session tracking, streaks, and historical charts.

  • Session timer: Press Ctrl+Shift+S (or the play button in the progress card) to start a writing session. Stop it to record your word-count delta and session length.
  • Daily & weekly goals: Set targets under Configurações → Writing Goals. The Painel ring shows today's progress at a glance.
  • Streak: Consecutive-day writing count at the top of the Progress view. Write at least one word on any day to keep the streak alive. Days follow your local timezone.
  • Velocity chart: 30-day rolling bar chart — words written per day. Hover a bar to see the exact count and which session contributed most.
  • 12-week heatmap: A GitHub-style contribution grid for the last 84 days. Darker cells indicate higher-output days.
  • Exportar history: Click Exportar CSV in the Progress view to download your full writing history for spreadsheet analysis.
help.analysis.progress.title: Progresso e legibilidade
help.article.selectPrompt: Select an article on the left to read it, or ask our IA assistant for help.
help.description: Find answers, learn how to master StoryCraft Studio, or ask our IA assistant for help.
help.docs.architecture.content:
Application Architecture

StoryCraft Studio is an offline-first Progressive Web App — a React 19 SPA with Google Gemini IA integration, IndexedDB persistence, and an optional Tauri v2 desktop shell. There is no backend server; API keys are entered in the UI and encrypted at rest.

Frontend Stack

  • React 19 with concurrent rendering and React.lazy() for all 14 views.
  • TypeScript in strict mode — exactOptionalPropertyTypes, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, noImplicitReturns.
  • Vite 8 for development server (HMR) and production builds with manual code-splitting.
  • Tailwind CSS 4.x with a custom semantic token layer (--sc-* CSS variables) for themeable design.
  • Redux Toolkit 2.x for global state — feature-sliced, with redux-undo wrapping the project slice (100-step history).
  • Zustand for transient UI state (command palette open, cross-project search, Flow Mode) that does not need Redux overhead.

Service Layer

  • IA services: Vercel IA SDK as the primary streaming layer; geminiService.ts for legacy thunks. Multi-provider abstraction in aiProviderService.ts supports Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, Grok, Ollama, WebLLM, ONNX, and Transformers.js.
  • Storage: Decomposed IDB layer under services/storage/ — separate modules for project data, snapshots, API keys, Codex, assets, and encryption.
  • Collaboration: Yjs CRDT + packages/collab-transport (vendor fork of y-webrtc with E2E RTCDataChannel encryption baked in).
  • Analytics: DuckDB-WASM worker in OPFS for advanced queries; the RAG vector store (384-dim float embeddings) lives there too.

Code Splitting Strategy

The entry bundle stays lean. Vite manual chunks separate: vendor-react, vendor-redux, plot-board (canvas/SVG), export-docx (docx/jsPDF/jszip), collab-yjs. DuckDB, ONNX, and WebLLM chunks are excluded from the Service Worker precache to avoid precaching large WASM modules.

Tauri Desktop Shell

An optional Rust/Tauri v2 wrapper adds native filesystem access, Ollama on localhost, window-state persistence, a native menu bar, and a built-in auto-updater. The web app and the desktop app share the same React codebase; runtime detection (isTauriRuntime()) gates Tauri-specific paths.
help.docs.architecture.title: Arquitetura de aplicativo
help.docs.dataModel.content:
Project Data Model

All project data lives in a single StoryProject object in the Redux store, persisted to IndexedDB (or the Tauri app data directory) via storageService. The project slice is wrapped with redux-undo, giving you 100 steps of undo/redo history in memory.

Top-Level Fields

  • id, title, description: Project identity. The ID is a UUID generated on creation and never changes.
  • manuscript: Array of StorySection objects — each with an id, title, content (plain text), order, type (chapter / part / epilogue), and optional notes.
  • characters: Array of Personagem objects — name, aliases, backstory, personality, appearance, goals, flaws, relationships, IA-generated portrait URL.
  • worlds: Array of WorldBuilding objects — name, description, geography, culture, magic/technology system, timeline events, locations, atmosphere image URL.
  • objects: Props, factions, and groups with scene and character links (behind enableObjectsGroups flag).
  • plotConnections, plotSubplots: Edges and subplot tracks for the Quadro de Enredo canvas.
  • tensionOverrides: Manual tension-curve adjustments per section.
  • outline: IA-generated outline sections (title, description, order) — the source of truth for the Esboço Generator view.
  • projectGoals: Target word count and target date.
  • writingHistory: Array of WritingSession records (date, words written, duration) powering the Progress Tracker charts.

Persistence

On every save (debounced 2 s after the last change), the project slice is compressed with LZ-String and written to the app-data IDB object store under the key 'project'. Configurações are written separately under 'settings'. When enableIdbAtRestEncryption is on, each value is AES-256-GCM encrypted before storage.

Snapshot Format

Snapshots capture a deep clone of the project at a point in time. Auto-snapshots (up to 20, pruned FIFO) and manual snapshots (kept indefinitely) are stored in a separate scene-revisions IDB store. Each snapshot record has an id, label, createdAt, and the full project JSON as a compressed blob.
help.docs.dataModel.title: Modelo de dados do projeto
help.docs.deployment.content:
Opções de implantação

StoryCraft Studio é um aplicativo web estático – qualquer host que possa servir arquivos HTML/CSS/JS funcionará. A compilação de produção é gerada por vite build e colocada em dist/.

  • Vercel (primário): A implantação canônica ao vivo. Configurado via vercel.json; cada push para main aciona uma implantação automática. Compilação compatível com Edge disponível via pnpm run build:edge.
  • Páginas Cloudflare: usa o script build:pages e é configurado em wrangler.toml. Atende na rede de borda global da Cloudflare. Bom para usuários em regiões onde a latência do Vercel é maior.
  • Páginas do GitHub: Host estático secundário; implantado automaticamente a partir da ramificação gh-pages por meio do trabalho de implantação de CI. Disponível como substituto.
  • Auto-hospedado: execute pnpm run build, copie dist/ para qualquer servidor de arquivos estáticos (Nginx, Caddy, S3 + CloudFront). Nenhuma lógica do lado do servidor é necessária — tudo é executado no navegador.
  • Versões Tauri para desktop: criadas pelo fluxo de trabalho do Tauri CI em versões marcadas. A assinatura de código fica do lado do mantenedor. As verificações de atualização usam o plug-in atualizador Tauri apontando para o endpoint JSON das versões do GitHub.
  • Variáveis ​​de ambiente: Somente variáveis ​​com prefixo VITE_*são incorporadas no momento da construção. Nenhuma chave secreta é incluída no binário. Os usuários inserem suas próprias chaves de API na IU.
help.docs.deployment.title: Opções de implantação
help.docs.duckdb.content:
DuckDB-WASM Analytics

When the DuckDB analytics feature flag is on, StoryCraft spins up a DuckDB-WASM worker that stores rich analytics data in the browser's OPFS — a persistent, sandboxed storage not cleared by normal browser cache clears.

  • What it enables: Advanced writing analytics dashboards — velocity trends beyond the 30-day chart, character mention frequency, world reference heatmaps, and the RAG vector store (384-dim float embeddings) for faster retrieval.
  • Why WASM: DuckDB runs entirely in the browser as a WebAssembly module; no data is sent to a server. Queries run at native SQL speed directly on your local data.
  • Performance note: The DuckDB worker adds ~6 MB to initial load and uses ~50–150 MB of WASM heap depending on manuscript size. On devices with less than 4 GB RAM, prefer leaving this flag off.
  • Dual-write: When active, every save writes to both IndexedDB (fast, reliable) and DuckDB (for analytics queries). The write is asynchronous and does not block the editor.
  • Lazy loading: The DuckDB listener is dynamically imported — it does not load until the flag is on and the app restarts. Cold-start performance is unaffected when the flag is off.
  • Reset: If analytics data becomes stale, go to Configurações → Data → Reset DuckDB store to drop and recreate the DuckDB tables. Your manuscript data in IndexedDB is unaffected.
help.docs.duckdb.title: Análise DuckDB-WASM
help.docs.featureFlags.content:
Feature Flag System

StoryCraft Studio gates experimental or resource-intensive features behind twelve boolean flags in featureFlagsSlice. Flags are persisted to localStorage under the key storycraft-feature-flags and survive app restarts.

  • Where to toggle: Configurações → Experimental flags. Each flag has a title, a one-line description, and a resource hint (memory / CPU) when relevant.
  • Effect on the app: Flags gate sidebar navigation entries, Redux listeners (e.g. the DuckDB dual-write listener), and lazy-loaded bundles. Disabling a flag hides the UI and pauses the listener but does not delete its data.
  • Current flags: Codex auto-tracking, Story Bible advanced, Research binder, Compile wizard, Project health score, Cross-project search, App health panel, Quadro de Enredo v2, DuckDB analytics, Objects & groups, Mind maps, Personagem interviews.
  • Safe enable order: Start with lower-memory flags (Codex, Cross-project search) before enabling WASM-heavy ones (Quadro de Enredo v2, DuckDB analytics). Always create a snapshot first in case a WASM flag triggers an out-of-memory restart on low-end hardware.
  • Resetting flags: If the app becomes unstable, go to Configurações → Data → Reset feature flags to restore all flags to their off defaults without touching project data.
help.docs.featureFlags.title: Sistema de sinalização de recursos
help.docs.lazyLoading.content:
Lazy Carregando & Bundle Architecture

StoryCraft Studio is engineered for a fast initial load. All 14 major views and several heavy libraries are loaded on-demand, only when first needed.

  • Views: Every view is wrapped in React.lazy() with a Suspense boundary. The view bundle downloads the first time you navigate to it; subsequent visits use the browser cache.
  • Vite manual chunks: The build splits vendor code into named chunks: vendor-react, vendor-redux, plot-board (canvas + SVG), export-docx (docx/jsPDF/jszip), collab-yjs (Yjs + y-webrtc). These download only when you first open their respective view.
  • IA provider layer: services/ai/index.ts is dynamically imported the first time an IA tool is used. The Vercel IA SDK and provider adapters (~200 KB gzipped) are not bundled in the entry chunk.
  • DuckDB & RAG: The DuckDB listener and local embedding model are loaded by the Redux listener middleware only when their feature flags are on. They do not contribute to cold-start bundle size.
  • Force graph: react-force-graph-2d is lazy-imported only when you navigate to the Personagem Graph and have at least one character — the empty-state view loads without triggering the import.
  • Bundle budget: The CI bundle:budget job enforces a maximum of 7 000 KB for the vendor chunk and 4 500 KB for the entry chunk. PRs that exceed these limits fail the build.
help.docs.lazyLoading.title: Carregamento lento e pacotes
help.docs.privacySecurity.content:
Privacy & Security Model

StoryCraft Studio is designed local-first: your story never leaves your device unless you explicitly send a prompt to a cloud IA provider.

  • No account required: There is no sign-up, no cloud sync, and no server that stores your manuscripts. All data lives in your browser's IndexedDB and OPFS.
  • API key encryption: When you enter an API key, it is encrypted with AES-256-GCM (256-bit key, random 12-byte IV, PBKDF2 with 310 000 SHA-256 iterations) before being stored in IndexedDB. The plaintext key is never written to disk or localStorage.
  • IA requests: Only the text you explicitly submit (e.g. a selected passage for "Improve Text") is sent to your chosen provider. The RAG pipeline runs locally; only the final assembled prompt travels over the network.
  • Content Security Policy: The web build includes a strict CSP that blocks inline scripts, arbitrary network requests, and localhost connections (preventing a compromised extension from accessing Ollama via the page).
  • Tauri desktop: The Rust shell restricts plugin permissions to the app data directory. Arbitrary filesystem access is blocked; the dialog plugin requires user confirmation for every open/save operation.
  • Collaboration: When Collaboration is enabled, Yjs updates are end-to-end encrypted (AES-256-GCM + PBKDF2) before leaving the browser. The signaling server never sees plaintext document content.
  • Dependency audits: OSV + CodeQL scanning runs on every CI push. Dependabot watches for new CVEs; override pins in pnpm.overrides are used when a patched version is not yet available upstream.
help.docs.privacySecurity.title: Modelo de privacidade e segurança
help.docs.pwaDesktop.content:
PWA & Desktop Packaging

StoryCraft Studio ships as both a Progressive Web App (PWA) and a native desktop app (Tauri). Both options keep your data local — no account required.

PWA (browser)

  • Install: Visit the web app in Chrome, Edge, or Safari and click the "Install" prompt in the address bar. The app icon appears on your desktop or home screen.
  • Offline shell cache: The Service Worker pre-caches the app shell (HTML, CSS, JS entry chunks) so the interface loads instantly, even offline. Only cloud IA requests need a network connection.
  • Storage: Data lives in IndexedDB and OPFS — persistent, sandboxed, and not cleared by normal browser cache clears.
  • Icons: The PWA manifest includes 192×192 and 512×512 maskable PNG icons used by Android and Windows for the installed app shortcut.

Tauri desktop app

  • What it adds: Native filesystem access, Ollama on localhost, window-state persistence (size, position), a File/Ajuda menu bar, and an optional auto-updater banner under Configurações → About.
  • Rust plugins bundled: fs, dialog, http, shell, updater, plus optional menu, tray, and window-state.
  • Data folder: On the desktop app, Configurações → Data → Open data folder reveals the OS path where all IndexedDB and OPFS data is stored — safe to back up manually.
  • Installers: Built by the Tauri CI workflow on tagged releases. Available for macOS (.dmg), Windows (.msi), and Linux (.AppImage / .deb).
help.docs.pwaDesktop.title: PWA e empacotamento de desktop
help.docs.ragPipeline.content:
RAG & Prompt Assembly

StoryCraft's retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipeline enriches every IA request with relevant passages from your own manuscript — so the model knows your story before it writes the next line.

  • Building the index: Go to Configurações → Advanced IA → Rebuild local search index. StoryCraft splits your manuscript into overlapping ~200-token chunks and encodes each with MiniLM-L6-v2 (384-dimensional embeddings) running locally via Transformers.js.
  • Hybrid retrieval: When a request is made, the pipeline scores candidate chunks three ways — semantic cosine similarity (~60%), lexical keyword overlap (~30%), and recency (favoring later chapters, ~10%) — then selects the top-K passages.
  • Prompt assembly: assembleRAGPrompt() builds a token-budgeted context block from the selected chunks and prepends it to the IA prompt. The chunk badge in the Writer panel shows how many passages were injected.
  • Where RAG is used: Continuar Writing, Brainstorm, IA Critic, and Quadro de Enredo "Suggest next beat" all use the same pipeline when RAG is enabled.
  • Rebuild triggers: Rebuild the index after importing a backup, adding many new characters, or when IA completions seem unfamiliar with your story's details.
  • Privacy: The index lives entirely in your browser's OPFS. Manuscrito text is never uploaded to build the index — only the final assembled prompt travels to your chosen cloud provider.
help.docs.ragPipeline.title: RAG e montagem imediata
help.docs.tauriDesktop.content:
Tauri Desktop App

The StoryCraft Studio desktop app wraps the same React codebase in a native Tauri v2 shell (Rust). It adds capabilities that browsers cannot provide while keeping your data fully local.

What the Desktop App Adds

  • Native filesystem access: Read and write files directly via the Tauri fs plugin — no browser file picker required for every operation. Logs are written to $APPDATA/logs/storycraft-YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl.
  • Ollama on localhost: Cloud IA providers block localhost connections in the browser (CSP); the desktop app bypasses this restriction, letting Ollama serve models at localhost:11434 without a proxy.
  • Window-state persistence: Window size, position, and maximized state are restored exactly on each launch via the Tauri window-state plugin.
  • Native menu bar: A File / Editar / View / Ajuda menu bar following OS conventions (macOS: menu in toolbar; Windows/Linux: embedded in the window).
  • Auto-updater: The Tauri updater plugin checks the GitHub releases JSON endpoint on startup and shows a banner under Configurações → About when a new version is available. Click Install update to download and apply it in the background.
  • Open data folder: Configurações → Data → Open data folder opens the OS file explorer at the directory where IndexedDB and OPFS data are stored — useful for manual backups.
  • Stronghold (optional): The tauri-plugin-stronghold can store the IDB encryption passphrase in the OS keychain so the unlock modal never appears on desktop.

Installers & Distribution

The Tauri CI workflow builds platform-specific installers on every tagged release (v*): .dmg for macOS (code-signed), .msi / .exe for Windows (code-signed), .AppImage and .deb for Linux. Installers are attached to GitHub Releases and referenced by the auto-updater endpoint.

Data Location

On desktop, data lives in the Tauri app data directory — typically %APPDATA%\StoryCraft Studio on Windows, ~/Library/Application Support/StoryCraft Studio on macOS, and ~/.local/share/storycraft-studio on Linux. You can safely copy this directory for a full manual backup.
help.docs.tauriDesktop.title: Aplicativo de desktop Tauri
help.faq.api.content:
Yes. To use any of the IA-powered features (like generation, improvement, or brainstorming), the application requires a Google Gemini API key. You can obtain a free key from Google IA Studio.

Go to Configurações → IA Configuration and enter your key. It will be encrypted using AES-256-GCM and stored securely in your browser's IndexedDB. Your key never leaves your device and is never transmitted to any server.
help.faq.api.title: Preciso de uma chave de API?
help.faq.offline.content:
Working Offline

StoryCraft Studio is local-first. Almost everything works without an internet connection once the app is loaded.

  • Always available offline: Writing, the Quadro de Enredo, character and world editing, snapshot creation and restore, Exportar to PDF / Markdown / TXT, all Configurações.
  • Needs network: Cloud IA providers (Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, Grok) send your prompt over the internet. Writing is never blocked — only IA features return an error when offline.
  • Pre-download local models: Go to Configurações → Advanced IA → Local IA models and download a WebLLM or ONNX model while online. Once cached, inference runs fully offline.
  • PWA shell cache: Install StoryCraft as a PWA (browser "Install" prompt) to cache the app shell via the Service Worker. Subsequent loads work offline even without internet.
  • OPFS storage: DuckDB analytics and the local embedding model use the browser Origin Private File System (OPFS) — a persistent, sandboxed area not cleared by normal cache-clearing actions.
help.faq.offline.title: Posso trabalhar off-line?
help.faq.privacy.content: Absolutely. Your privacy is paramount. Because all data is stored locally in your browser, we never have access to your work. Your story remains completely private and under your control. When you use IA features, only the specific text needed for that request is sent to the Google Gemini API, and it is not used to train their models.
help.faq.privacy.title: Minha história é privada?
help.faq.providers.content:
Which IA Provider Should I Use?

StoryCraft supports multiple IA providers. Here is a quick guide to help you choose.

  • Google Gemini (cloud): The default. Gemini Flash is fast and free-tier friendly; Gemini Pro gives higher quality. Requires a free API key from Google IA Studio. Best for: everyday writing assistance.
  • OpenAI (cloud): GPT-4o and GPT-4o-mini via API key. Strong instruction-following and prose rewriting. Best for: users already on the OpenAI ecosystem.
  • Ollama (local — desktop only): Runs models on your machine via localhost:11434. Requires the Tauri desktop app. Best for: maximum privacy and zero API cost with any Ollama-supported model.
  • WebLLM (local — browser): GPU inference directly in the browser; no server, no API key. Models are downloaded once and cached. Best for: privacy without the desktop app, fully offline after first download.
  • Hybrid fallback: Enable under Configurações → Advanced IA to chain providers automatically — e.g., try Gemini first, fall back to Ollama on error. Useful for resilient workflows.
help.faq.providers.title: Which IA provider should I use?
help.faq.saving.content:
Your entire project, including all text and IA-generated images, is saved automatically and continuously in your web browser's local storage (a database called IndexedDB). This means your work is persisted on your computer between sessions. A 'Saving...' indicator appears in the header when changes are being written, followed by 'All changes saved'.

There is no cloud account or server-side storage. This ensures complete privacy but also means you are responsible for creating backups using the 'Exportar Backup' feature in Configurações.
help.faq.saving.title: Como os dados do meu projeto são salvos?
help.gettingStarted.desktop.content:
Where you run StoryCraft

Browser / PWA: Install from the browser for offline cache; data stays in IndexedDB.

Desktop (Tauri): Native FS access, Ollama to localhost, optional auto-updater, window state restore, and “open data folder” in Configurações → Data.

API keys and manuscripts never leave your device unless you send text to a cloud IA provider.
help.gettingStarted.desktop.title: Aplicativo de desktop (Tauri) e PWA
help.gettingStarted.firstProject.content:
Choose Your Path

The Bem-vindo Portal gives you three powerful ways to begin:

  1. Start with a Template: This is great for structured storytelling. Go to the Templates view, choose a structure like the 'Three-Act Structure,' and use the 'Personalize with IA' feature to get custom prompts for each section based on your story idea.
  2. Gerar with IA: If you have a concept but no structure, use the Esboço Generator. Just provide your genre and a story idea, and it will create a full, editable plot outline that you can apply to your manuscript.
  3. Start Blank: For those who prefer a completely open canvas, this option creates a new project with an empty first chapter, ready for you to start writing immediately.
help.gettingStarted.firstProject.title: Iniciando seu primeiro projeto
help.gettingStarted.navigation.content:
Every major view

  • Painel — goals, stats, backup quick actions, onboarding tour.
  • Manuscrito — three-panel editor with @mentions and #world tags.
  • IA Writing Studio — continue, improve, dialogue, RAG context.
  • Quadro de Cenas / Quadro de Enredo — canvas, swimlanes, tension curve, IA beat suggestions.
  • Characters, Worlds, Objects, Mind Maps, Interviews — worldbuilding suite (some behind feature flags).
  • Personagem Graph, Consistency Checker, Critic — analysis tools.
  • Exportar, Configurações, Ajuda — publishing, configuration, learning center.
help.gettingStarted.navigation.title: Visão geral da barra lateral e visualizações
help.gettingStarted.palette.content:
Fast navigation

Press Ctrl+K (Windows/Linux) or Cmd+K (macOS) to open the command palette. Pesquisar views, IA actions, theme toggles, and pinned favorites. Right-click a command to pin it.

Customize chords under Configurações → Shortcuts. Conflicts are highlighted in amber.
help.gettingStarted.palette.title: Paleta de comandos e atalhos
help.gettingStarted.tour.content:
Bem-vindo to StoryCraft Studio!

The main navigation is on the left sidebar, giving you access to all core tools:

  • Painel: Your project's home base. Track stats, set goals, and see your project at a glance.
  • Manuscrito: A focused, three-panel view for writing and organizing your chapters.
  • IA Writing Studio: Your creative partner for generating text, improving prose, and overcoming writer's block.
  • Templates: Jumpstart your story with pre-built narrative structures.
  • Esboço Generator: Brainstorm a complete plot from a simple idea using IA.
  • Characters & Mundo Building: Criar and manage your story's assets with rich details and IA-generated art.
  • Exportar: Prepare your finished work for sharing in various formats.
  • Configurações: Customize your workspace and manage your project data.
help.gettingStarted.tour.title: Um tour rápido pela interface
help.management.backupDashboard.content:
Painel Backup Quick Actions

The Backup card on the Painel gives you one-click access to the most common data-safety actions without navigating away from your project overview.

  • Exportar JSON: Click Exportar backup to download a complete JSON snapshot of your entire project — all manuscript text, characters, worlds, settings, and scene board layout. Use this to move the project to another device or as a manual archive.
  • Import backup: Click Import backup to load a previously exported JSON file. This replaces the current project, so a confirmation dialog appears first.
  • Latest snapshot: The card shows the name and timestamp of the most recent auto-snapshot. Click it to open the Version Control panel where you can compare, restore, or label that snapshot.
  • Jump to full settings: The Configurações → link at the bottom of the card takes you directly to Configurações → Backup & Data for advanced options: auto-snapshot interval, encryption passphrase, encrypted library ZIP export, and cross-project index rebuild.
  • Snapshot count: A small badge shows how many snapshots exist. Auto-snapshots beyond the limit of 20 are pruned oldest-first; manual snapshots are never pruned automatically.
help.management.backupDashboard.title: Painel backup quick actions
help.management.collaboration.content:
Collaboration (Experimental)

Real-time collaborative editing via Yjs and y-webrtc. Multiple writers can edit the same manuscript simultaneously with automatic conflict resolution. Enable the feature flag, then configure under Configurações → Collaboration.

  • Signaling server: Enter a WebRTC signaling URL in Configurações → Collaboration. Use the public wss://signaling.yjs.dev for testing, or host your own server.
  • Room ID: Share the room ID with co-authors. All writers in the same room ID connect automatically. The room is identified by a SHA-256 hash of the project and room name.
  • E2E encryption: Set a room password to enable end-to-end encryption. All Yjs updates are encrypted before leaving the browser. The green lock badge in the panel confirms encryption is active.
  • Cursor positions: Other writers' cursors appear as coloured carets in the Manuscrito editor in real time.
  • Limitations: Sessions are peer-to-peer — no central server holds the document. Offline sync (merge later) is not yet supported. If all peers disconnect, the session ends.
help.management.collaboration.title: Colaboração (experimental)
help.management.data.content:
Your Data, Your Control

You can manage your project data in Configurações > Data & Backups. StoryCraft Studio gives you full control:

  • Exportar Backup: This saves a complete JSON backup file of your entire project (including all text and settings) to your computer. Use this to move your project to another device.
  • Import Backup: Load a project from a JSON backup file.
  • Reset Project: This will permanently delete all your data in the browser and start a new, blank project.
  • Project Snapshots: This powerful feature is your safety net. The app automatically takes snapshots of your project periodically. You can also create a named snapshot manually before making major changes. You can restore your project to any snapshot at any time.
help.management.data.title: Gerenciamento de dados e backups
help.management.export.content:
From Draft to Document

The Exportar view is a full publishing suite. You have a live preview of your formatted document on the right. In the 'Exportar Controls' panel, you can:

  • Select Content: Choose which parts of your project to include, such as Personagem Dossiers or Mundo-Building notes.
  • Choose a Format: We support Markdown (.md), Plain Text (.txt), and PDF (.pdf).
  • Customize PDF: For PDFs, you can set the font, font size, line spacing, and choose whether to include a professional title page.
  • Use IA Enhancements: Check the 'Gerar IA Synopsis' box to have the IA write a professional one-page summary of your entire manuscript to be included in the export.
help.management.export.title: Exportando seu trabalho
help.management.versionControl.content:
Version Control Panel

The Version Control panel (header icon or Ctrl+Shift+V) lets you manage project snapshots like lightweight commits — no branch merging, no remote server.

  • Auto-snapshots: Created every 30 minutes of active editing. Up to 20 auto-snapshots are kept; older ones are pruned automatically (FIFO).
  • Manual snapshots: Click Criar snapshot, enter a descriptive name (e.g., "Before Act 2 rewrite"), and confirm. Manual snapshots are never pruned automatically.
  • Diff view: Select any snapshot and click View diff to see a word-level diff against the current manuscript. Added text is green; removed text is red. Only changed sections are shown.
  • Restore: Click Restore on any snapshot to rewind to that point. A confirmation dialog lists what will change; a safety auto-snapshot of the current state is created first.
  • Branches: Click New branch to fork the current snapshot into an independent experimental branch. Switch branches from the panel header dropdown.
help.management.versionControl.title: Painel de controle de versão
help.noResults: Nenhum resultado encontrado para "{{query}}". Tente outra pesquisa.
help.proTips.featureFlags.content:
Feature Flag Rollout Strategy

Feature flags let you try powerful experimental features without committing permanently. Here is a safe rollout workflow.

  1. Criar a snapshot first: Before enabling any flag, go to Configurações → Backup → Criar snapshot and give it a descriptive name. If a flag causes instability, you have a safe restoration point.
  2. Enable one at a time: Toggle a single flag, use the feature for at least one writing session, then enable the next. This makes it easy to pinpoint which flag caused unexpected behavior.
  3. Low-memory flags first: Codex auto-tracking, Cross-project search, and Personagem interviews add minimal overhead. Enable these freely on any device.
  4. WASM-heavy flags on capable hardware: Quadro de Enredo v2 adds canvas rendering; DuckDB analytics loads a ~6 MB WASM module using ~50–150 MB heap. On devices with less than 4 GB RAM, enable these one at a time and monitor Configurações → Performance for memory pressure.
  5. Combining flags: Quadro de Enredo v2 + DuckDB analytics together increase WASM memory significantly. If you combine them, enable the Performance → Low-memory mode toggle to reduce DuckDB's buffer pool and Quadro de Enredo's render resolution.
  6. Resetting flags: If the app becomes sluggish, go to Configurações → Data → Reset feature flags to turn all flags off without deleting project data. Then re-enable flags one by one.
help.proTips.featureFlags.title: Estratégia de implementação de sinalizadores de recursos
help.proTips.mentions.content:
Criar a Living Document

The @mention system in the Manuscrito Editor is more than just a visual aid; it's a way to create a deeply interconnected story bible. By consistently tagging characters with @ and worlds with #, you create a visual map of how your story elements interact within the narrative. This makes it easy to see which characters are active in a scene or where your story is set at a glance. It's a simple habit that pays huge dividends during editing and revision.
help.proTips.mentions.title: Usando @menções de forma eficaz
help.proTips.snapshots.content:
Write Without Fear

The Snapshot system is your best friend during heavy revisions. Here's a pro workflow:

  1. Before starting a major rewrite of a chapter, go to Configurações > Data & Backups.
  2. Click 'Criar Snapshot' and give it a descriptive name, like 'Pre-Act 2 Rewrite'.
  3. Now, write and revise freely, knowing you can always return to that safe version.
  4. The app also creates automatic snapshots every 30 minutes of activity, so you're always covered. You can restore from any automatic or manual snapshot if you change your mind about a direction your story has taken.
help.proTips.snapshots.title: Estratégias instantâneas para escrita segura
help.proTips.workflow.content:
Recommended Writing Workflow

This five-step process makes the most of every feature in StoryCraft Studio.

  1. Plan (Esboço or Template): Start in the Esboço Generator with your genre and concept, or pick a story template. Use IA personalisation to populate chapter prompts, then apply to the manuscript.
  2. Populate (Manuscrito): Write chapter by chapter. @mention characters and #worlds as you go to build a live cross-reference map.
  3. Draft (IA Writing Studio): Enable RAG context so IA completions draw from your actual manuscript. Use Continuar Writing for momentum; Brainstorm when stuck on what comes next.
  4. Structure (Quadro de Enredo): Switch to Swimlanes view for a three-act overview. Drag beat cards to rebalance pacing and review the tension curve.
  5. Deliver (Exportar + Backup): Use the Exportar view for PDF or Markdown output. Criar a manual snapshot, then export a JSON backup before sharing.

Tip: Criar a named snapshot before every major rewrite so you can always roll back.
help.proTips.workflow.title: Fluxo de trabalho de escrita recomendado
help.settingsGuide.accessibility.content:
Accessibility Hub

StoryCraft Studio is built to meet WCAG 2.2 AA. The Accessibility Hub gives you both quick presets and granular toggles so you can tune the interface to your exact needs.

Presets

Four curated profiles apply multiple settings at once — activate one as a starting point, then adjust individual toggles:

  • Motor comfort: Large text on, focus indicators on, comfortable touch targets on (minimum 44px). Ideal for users with limited fine-motor control or on touch devices.
  • Low Vision: High contrast on, large text on, focus indicators on. Increases color contrast ratios above WCAG AA minimum across all UI surfaces.
  • Cognitive Load: Reduced motion on, focus indicators on, live region verbosity set to Minimal. Reduces animation and non-essential announcements.
  • Screen Reader: Screen reader mode on, focus indicators on, live region verbosity set to Verbose. Ensures all dynamic content is announced to assistive technology.

Individual Toggles

  • High contrast: Activates a higher-contrast token set across all surfaces. Does not use the OS-level forced-colors API — the contrast mode is StoryCraft-specific so themes remain consistent.
  • Reduced motion: Disables CSS transitions, the Quadro de Enredo canvas animations, and rAF-based UI micro-animations. Respects the prefers-reduced-motion media query by default; this toggle overrides per-user preference independently of OS settings.
  • Large text: Scales body text and heading type up one step using the fluid type scale (--text-sc-* tokens). Does not affect the manuscript editor font size, which is controlled separately under Configurações → Editor.
  • Screen reader announcements: Enables a persistent ARIA live region that announces status changes (auto-save, IA progress, collaboration events). Set verbosity to Verbose for maximum coverage.
  • Focus indicators: Ensures all interactive elements show a focus-visible:ring-2 outline. Enabled by default; disable only if you rely on custom OS focus styles.
  • Comfortable touch targets: Adds extra padding around small buttons and icon controls to meet the 44×44px minimum touch target size recommended by WCAG 2.5.5.
  • Color-blind mode: Applies one of four OpenDyslexic-inspired color shifts — Protanopia, Deuteranopia, Tritanopia, or Monochromacy. Adjusts the semantic state colors (success, warning, error, info) to remain distinguishable in each mode.
help.settingsGuide.accessibility.title: Centro de acessibilidade
help.settingsGuide.ai.content:
IA & Advanced IA Configurações

Configure everything about how StoryCraft Studio generates content — from which provider handles requests to how local retrieval is assembled.

  • Default provider & model: Choose between Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, Grok, Ollama, WebLLM, ONNX, or Transformers.js. The selected provider is used for all IA Writing Studio tools unless overridden per-session.
  • Creativity (temperature): Slide from 0 (deterministic, precise) to 1 (highly creative, unpredictable). For brainstorming use 0.8–1.0; for factual summaries use 0.2–0.4.
  • Hybrid fallback chain: Define an ordered list of providers. If the primary provider returns an error, StoryCraft retries with the next in the chain. Useful for resilient setups.
  • RAG index mode: Hybrid blends semantic embeddings (MiniLM-L6-v2, 384 dimensions) with lexical overlap and recency weighting. Lexical is faster and works on devices without a capable GPU.
  • Rebuild local search index: Rebuilds the in-browser embedding index from scratch. Do this after importing a backup or after adding many new characters and world entries.
  • DuckDB analytics toggle: Enables the OPFS-backed DuckDB-WASM worker for advanced writing analytics dashboards. Adds ~6 MB to first-run WASM load — leave off on low-memory devices.
  • GPU metrics panel: Shows real-time VRAM usage and WebGPU adapter info. Useful when running WebLLM to confirm the model fits in your GPU's memory.
help.settingsGuide.ai.title: IA & Advanced IA settings
help.settingsGuide.backup.content:
Backup & Data Configurações

Two related settings categories: Backup for snapshot automation and encryption, and Data for exporting, importing, and resetting project data.

Backup

  • Auto-snapshot interval: Choose how often the app creates automatic snapshots — 15 min, 30 min (default), or 60 min. Auto-snapshots are kept up to a maximum of 20; older ones are pruned FIFO. Manual snapshots are never pruned.
  • Encryption passphrase: Set a passphrase to encrypt exported JSON and library ZIP files. Encrypted exports require the same passphrase to import — store it safely. Leaving this blank means exports are unencrypted JSON.

Data

  • Exportar JSON backup: Downloads a full project JSON file (optionally encrypted if a passphrase is set).
  • Import backup: Loads a previously exported JSON — encrypted or plain. Replaces the current project after confirmation.
  • Exportar encrypted library ZIP: Packages all browser projects into a single AES-256-GCM encrypted archive — useful for full device migration.
  • Reset project: Wipes all manuscripts, characters, worlds, and settings from IndexedDB. An auto-snapshot is created first for safety.
  • Open data folder (desktop): On the Tauri desktop app, opens the OS file explorer at the directory where IndexedDB and OPFS files are stored.
help.settingsGuide.backup.title: Backup e dados
help.settingsGuide.flags.content:
Experimental Feature Flags

StoryCraft Studio ships 19 feature flags for capabilities in active development. Flags are stored in storycraft-feature-flags in localStorage and survive app restarts. Criar a snapshot before enabling WASM-heavy flags on low-memory devices.

Always-On Features

  • Codex auto-tracking (default: on): Automatically extracts character names, world references, and object mentions from your manuscript as you write and updates the Codex index in the background.
  • Cross-project search (default: on): Indexes all projects in your browser for instant full-text search across manuscripts, characters, and worlds. Uses IndexedDB — no data leaves your device.

Writing & Structure

  • Quadro de Enredo v2 (deprecated UI flag — legacy compat only): The v2 Quadro de Enredo is the current board; this flag is retained for localStorage backward compatibility. It is hidden from the Configurações UI.
  • Story Bible advanced: Unlocks the advanced Story Bible view with relationship maps, timeline threading, and multi-world atlas navigation.
  • Research binder: Adds a dedicated Research Binder view for storing external links, reference notes, and clippings alongside your manuscript.
  • Compile wizard: A step-by-step wizard for assembling a final manuscript from selected sections, with chapter reordering, front matter, and export settings in one flow.

IA & Analytics

  • DuckDB analytics: Enables the WASM-based DuckDB worker for advanced writing analytics dashboards — velocity trends, character mention frequency, and the RAG vector store. Adds ~6 MB to first-run load; uses ~50–150 MB WASM heap.
  • Project health score: Shows a 0–100 blended quality indicator on the Painel: goal completeness, cast depth, world-building coverage, and Consistency Checker warnings.
  • App health panel: Adds a System panel under Configurações showing IndexedDB usage, OPFS storage, memory pressure, SW registration status, and DuckDB worker health — useful for diagnosing slowdowns.

Worldbuilding Suite

  • Objects & groups: Track props, factions, and grouped entities with scene and character links. Adds an Objects view to the sidebar.
  • Mind maps: A free-canvas visual tool for exploring relationships between story elements. Drag-and-drop nodes, label edges, export as PNG.
  • Personagem interviews: Structured IA-powered Q&A sessions that reveal a character's voice and backstory. Answers can be applied directly to the character dossier.

Collaboration & Platform (Beta)

  • Voice support: Enables the voice command interface — push-to-talk, dictation into the manuscript editor, and natural-language navigation commands.
  • Cloud sync (stub — not yet active): Placeholder for future Cloudflare R2 sync. AES-256-GCM E2E encrypted; API keys never leave the browser.
  • LoRA adapters (stub): Inference-side wiring for fine-tuned Ollama LoRA adapters. Training remains a Python sidecar workflow.
  • Plugin system (beta): Sandboxed third-party plugins via a capability-scoped API. Reference plugins (word-count overlay, scene appender) ship with the app.
  • Objects & groups, Mind maps, Personagem interviews: See Worldbuilding Suite above.
  • RTL layout (beta stub — ar/he): Activates html[dir=rtl] and BiDi layout. Arabic and Hebrew locale stubs exist; full translation content is a community milestone for v2.0.

Encryption (Security)

  • IDB at-rest encryption: Enables passphrase-derived AES-256-GCM encryption for all IndexedDB stores. Set your passphrase under Configurações → Privacy → Encrypt project data at rest. On each startup a modal prompts for the passphrase; the key is session-scoped and never written to disk.
  • Voice WASM (B-2 scaffold — model download not yet wired): Whisper.cpp WASM STT and Silero VAD v4 engines. The scaffold is complete; the model-download UI is a Phase 3 task.
help.settingsGuide.flags.title: Todos os 12 sinalizadores de recursos
help.settingsGuide.overview.content:
Configurações at a Glance

StoryCraft Studio's Configurações are organized into semantic groups accessible from the left-hand navigation panel. Use the search box at the top of Configurações to jump directly to any category by keyword.

Setting Groups

  • General — UI language, region, and PWA installation. Change the display language here; it takes effect immediately without a reload.
  • Appearance & Themes — Light, Dark, Sepia, and specialty themes (Midnight, Forest, Ember, Ocean, Arctic, Fantasy, Romance). Each activates a full semantic-token palette across the UI.
  • Editor — Font family (Serif, Sans-serif, Monospace, Dyslexia-friendly), base font size, line spacing, paragraph indent, and writing focus mode settings.
  • IA Models — Primary IA provider and model (Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, Grok, Ollama, WebLLM, ONNX, Transformers.js), API keys, creativity slider, and hybrid fallback chain.
  • Advanced IA — RAG index mode (Hybrid / Lexical), local IA model download, DuckDB analytics toggle, GPU metrics panel, and prompt customization.
  • Accessibility — Presets and granular toggles for high contrast, reduced motion, large text, screen reader announcements, focus rings, comfortable touch targets, and color-blind modes.
  • Privacy & Data — IA request policy (local-only vs. cloud), IDB at-rest encryption passphrase, GDPR toggles, analytics opt-out.
  • Performance — Auto-save interval, cache budget, preload and lazy-load preferences, low-memory mode.
  • Notifications — Desktop notifications, writing reminders, goal achievement alerts, and collaboration update badges.
  • Collaboration — WebRTC signaling URLs, room sharing, and E2E-encryption room password.
  • Integrations — LanguageTool (grammar checker), cloud sync providers, and third-party export targets.
  • Backup & Snapshots — Auto-snapshot interval (15 / 30 / 60 min), manual snapshot creation, snapshot limit, and backup encryption passphrase.
  • Data — Exportar JSON backup, import backup, encrypted library ZIP, reset project, rebuild cross-project index, and reset DuckDB store.
  • Shortcuts — Record or reset keyboard bindings for every action; conflict detection highlights duplicate chords in amber.
  • Voice — Enable voice commands, choose STT / TTS engine, push-to-talk shortcut, cloud STT fallback, and dictation auto-punctuation.
  • About & Updates — App version, Tauri desktop auto-updater, open-source licenses, and data folder path (desktop only).
  • Experimental flags — 19 toggles for features in beta. Enable one at a time; create a snapshot first on low-memory devices.
help.settingsGuide.overview.title: Configurações overview
help.settingsGuide.pwaInstall.content:
Installing StoryCraft Studio

You can install StoryCraft Studio on your device for faster access, a dedicated window, and full offline use — no app store required. Go to Configurações → General and find the Install as App card.

When an Install Button appears

When you visit StoryCraft in Chrome, Edge, or Safari, the browser automatically evaluates whether the app is installable. If it is, a Install StoryCraft Studio button appears in Configurações → General. Clicking it triggers the browser's built-in install prompt.

When no button is shown

If no install button appears, install manually via the browser:

  • Chrome / Edge: Click the install icon (⊕) in the address bar, or open the browser menu (⋮) and click Install StoryCraft Studio.
  • Safari on iPhone/iPad: Tap the Share button (□↑), then tap Adicionar to Home Screen.
  • Firefox: Firefox does not support PWA installation directly; use Chrome or Edge for the installed-app experience.

After Installation

The app opens in its own window without browser chrome — tabs, address bar, and toolbar are hidden. It behaves exactly like the browser version: the same data, the same features, and the same Service Worker for offline caching. The install status is shown in Configurações → General with a green checkmark.

Clearing the Cache

The Clear app cache button in Configurações → General forces the Service Worker to re-download all assets on the next reload. Use it if the UI looks stale or you want to confirm you are running the latest version. The reload happens automatically after the cache is cleared.
help.settingsGuide.pwaInstall.title: Instalando StoryCraft como um aplicativo
help.settingsGuide.shortcuts.content:
Keyboard Shortcuts Configurações

The Shortcuts page lets you customize every keyboard binding in StoryCraft Studio, record new chords, and detect conflicts before they cause confusion.

  • Recording a shortcut: Click the binding field next to any action, then press the key combination you want. Single keys, Ctrl/Cmd combos, and multi-modifier chords (e.g. Ctrl+Shift+P) are all supported.
  • Conflict detection: If two actions share the same chord, both fields are highlighted in amber and a warning badge appears. The conflict must be resolved before the new binding is saved.
  • Resetting to defaults: Click Reset all shortcuts at the bottom of the page to restore built-in bindings. You can also reset individual actions by clicking the circular arrow icon next to each one.
  • Built-in defaults: Command palette Ctrl+K, save Ctrl+S, new section Ctrl+N, search Ctrl+F, export Ctrl+E, theme toggle Ctrl+Shift+T, version control panel Ctrl+Shift+V.
  • Context-sensitive bindings: Some shortcuts only fire inside a specific view (e.g. G to toggle graph/table in Personagem Graph). These are listed under their view's group on the shortcuts page.
  • Import / Exportar: The Exportar shortcuts button saves your custom bindings as a JSON file; Import shortcuts applies a previously exported set — useful for syncing across devices.
help.settingsGuide.shortcuts.title: Atalhos de teclado
help.title: Ajuda & Learning Center
help.worldbuilding.characters.content:
Breathing Life into Your Cast

In the Characters view, you manage your cast of characters. You can:

  • Adicionar Manually: Criar a blank character sheet to fill out yourself.
  • Gerar with IA: Provide a simple concept (e.g., 'a cynical detective who loves cats'), and the IA will generate a detailed backstory, motivation, personality, flaws, and more.
  • Gerar a Portrait: Based on the 'Appearance' field, the IA can generate a unique character portrait. You can then use the 'Refine Portrait' input to make changes (e.g., 'give him a scar over his left eye').
  • Regenerate Fields: Don't like a specific detail? Click the regenerate icon next to any field to have the IA rewrite just that part.
help.worldbuilding.characters.title: Construindo Personagens
help.worldbuilding.graph.content:
Personagem Relationship Graph

The Personagem Graph visualizes every character you have created as a node in a force-directed network. Edges between nodes represent the relationship type you defined on each character's dossier. Use it to spot isolated characters, tangled alliances, or missing antagonist arcs at a glance.

  • Drag nodes: Click and drag any node to reposition it. The physics simulation re-settles after you release.
  • Filter by tag: Use the tag filter dropdown at the top of the view to show only characters with a specific tag, e.g. "Protagonist" or "Antagonist". Unmatched nodes fade to grey.
  • Open a dossier: Click a node to highlight its direct connections; double-click to open the full character dossier panel on the right.
  • Keyboard alternative: Press G while the Personagem Graph view is active to toggle between the graph and a screen-reader-friendly relationship table that lists the same data in rows and columns.
  • Zoom & pan: Scroll to zoom; drag the canvas background to pan. Use Ctrl+0 to fit the entire graph to the viewport.
help.worldbuilding.graph.title: Personagem relationship graph
help.worldbuilding.interviews.content:
Personagem Interviews

Personagem Interviews guide you through structured IA-powered Q&A sessions that reveal a character's voice, backstory, and motivation from their own perspective. Enable under Configurações → Experimental flags → Personagem interviews.

  • Start an interview: Open a character's detail page and click Start Interview. Choose an archetype (Hero, Mentor, Shadow, etc.) for a curated question set.
  • Interview flow: The IA presents one question at a time and responds in the character's voice. Your follow-up notes steer the next question.
  • Sample questions: "What do you fear most?", "Describe the moment your life changed forever.", "What would you sacrifice to achieve your goal?"
  • Salvar to profile: Click Apply to Personagem at the end. Key answers are written back to the Backstory and Motivation fields automatically.
  • Re-interview: Run multiple sessions with different archetypes to explore new dimensions of the same character.
help.worldbuilding.interviews.title: Personagem interviews
help.worldbuilding.mindmaps.content:
Mind Maps

Mind Maps give you a visual canvas for exploring relationships between characters, scenes, worlds, and objects. Enable under Configurações → Experimental flags → Mind maps.

  • Node types: Adicionar character, world, scene, or free-text nodes. Each type has its own colour and icon for quick identification.
  • Links: Draw a connection from any node to another. Each link can carry a label (e.g., "allies with", "lives in"). Drag the midpoint to reroute the line.
  • Zoom & pan: Scroll to zoom; drag the canvas background to pan. Use the minimap in the lower-right corner to navigate large maps.
  • Filter: Click the filter toolbar to hide all nodes of a given type — useful for focusing on character relationships only or on location clusters.
  • Exportar: The toolbar Exportar button saves the current canvas as a PNG for sharing or printing.
help.worldbuilding.mindmaps.title: Mapas mentais
help.worldbuilding.objects.content:
Objects & Groups Inventory

Track every significant prop, faction, and grouped entity with full scene and character links. Enable under Configurações → Experimental flags → Objects & Groups.

  • Props: Physical items that appear in scenes — weapons, artefacts, vehicles, key items. Adicionar a description and link the prop to the scene where it first appears.
  • Factions: Organisations or power structures. Define goals and allegiances; characters can belong to multiple factions.
  • Groups: Custom collections spanning types — tag characters and locations under one named group to filter the inventory at a glance.
  • Scene & Personagem Links: Open any Object card and click Link to scene or Link to character. Linked entities appear as chips; click one to jump directly to the target.
  • Filter Bar: Use the type filter (Prop / Faction / Group) and the search bar at the top of the Objects view to narrow the list quickly.
help.worldbuilding.objects.title: Inventário de objetos e grupos
help.worldbuilding.worlds.content:
Constructing Your Universe

The Mundo Building view is for defining your story's setting. Similar to the character builder, you can:

  • Gerar with IA: Criar a rich world profile from a core concept (e.g., 'a steampunk city powered by captured lightning').
  • Flesh out Details: Manually define geography, culture, and magic/technology systems.
  • Criar Timelines and Locations: Use the dedicated tabs in the world atlas to add historical events to a timeline or define key locations within your world.
  • Gerar an Ambiance Image: Let the IA create a beautiful, atmospheric image based on your world's description to set the mood.
help.worldbuilding.worlds.title: Mundo Building
help.writing.bookPreview.content:
Book Preview

Book Preview renders your entire manuscript as a continuous reading experience — the closest view to a real book before you export.

  • Opening: Click the eye icon in the Manuscrito header, or use the Command Palette and search "Book Preview".
  • Live table of contents: The TOC panel on the left uses an IntersectionObserver to highlight the current chapter as you scroll. Click any chapter title to jump to it.
  • Reading controls: Adjust font size with the A− / A+ buttons in the Preview toolbar. The font family follows your Editor font preference from Configurações.
  • Fullscreen: Click the Expand button (or press F11 in most browsers) to enter fullscreen reading mode. Press Escape to exit.
  • Exportar preparation: Use Book Preview as a final read-through. The layout closely matches the PDF export output, so you can catch formatting issues before committing to a file.
  • Progress bar: A thin reading-progress bar at the top tracks how far through the manuscript you have scrolled.
help.writing.bookPreview.title: Visualização e progresso do livro
help.writing.manuscript.content:
A Distraction-Free Writing Environment

The Manuscrito view is designed for focus and flow. On desktop, it features three resizable panels:

  • Navigator (Left): This panel lists all your chapters or sections. Click to jump between them or drag-and-drop to reorder your story on the fly.
  • Editor (Center): This is your main writing space. It features real-time highlighting for mentions. Use the @ key to tag characters (e.g., @Jax) and the # key to tag worlds (e.g., #Neo-Veridia) for easy reference.
  • Inspector (Right): This panel keeps your project's high-level details, like the Title and Logline, always within reach. You can even use the IA Assistant here to generate logline ideas.

On mobile devices, the Navigator and Inspector are tucked away in drawers to maximize your writing space.
help.writing.manuscript.title: The Three-Panel Manuscrito Editor
help.writing.outline.content:
From Idea to Plot in Minutes

This is one of the most powerful tools for starting a story. The process is simple:

  1. Provide Your Idea: Enter a genre and your main concept.
  2. Use Advanced Options: For better results, specify key characters, setting, and desired pacing. You can even ask the IA to include a plot twist.
  3. Editar and Refine: The IA will produce a complete, multi-section outline. This is a fully interactive draft. You can rewrite titles and descriptions, drag sections to reorder them, regenerate individual sections, or add new ones manually.
  4. Apply to Manuscrito: When you're happy, click 'Apply Esboço to Manuscrito'. This will create a new manuscript with each outline section as a chapter, complete with your descriptions as writing prompts.
help.writing.outline.title: Mastering the IA Esboço Generator
help.writing.plotBoardV2.content:
Canvas Mode — Deep Dive

Canvas is the freeform mode of Quadro de Enredo v2. It is designed for visual thinkers who want to arrange story beats spatially rather than linearly.

Navigation

  • Pan: Drag the canvas background (not a card). Hold Space on desktop to temporarily switch to pan mode.
  • Zoom: Scroll the mouse wheel, pinch on touch screens, or use the + / − controls in the toolbar. Press Ctrl+0 to fit the entire board to the viewport.
  • Minimap: The minimap in the lower-right corner shows a bird's-eye view of all cards. Click inside the minimap to jump to any area of the board.

Cards and layout

  • Grid snap: Enable the grid toggle in the toolbar to snap cards to an 8px grid — keeps your board tidy when you have many beats.
  • Multi-select: Hold Shift and click multiple cards to move them as a group. Useful for repositioning a whole act.
  • Card colors: Set a card color to visually separate subplots, POV characters, or act acts. Colors persist with the card and appear in swimlane view too.

Connections

  • Drawing: Hover a card edge until the connector handle appears, then drag to another card. The SVG line curves automatically around intervening cards.
  • Labels: Click a connection line to add a label — e.g. "causes", "foreshadows", "mirrors".
  • Touch targets: Connection lines have a 44px invisible hit area so they remain tappable on phones without needing pixel-perfect aim.

IA beat suggestion

Click IA ✦ → Suggest next beat to have the IA propose a beat card using RAG context from your manuscript. The suggestion includes a title, description, and recommended act. Click Accept to create the card, or Suggest again to see another option.
help.writing.plotBoardV2.title: Quadro de Enredo v2 — canvas deep dive
help.writing.ragHybrid.content:
Hybrid RAG — Retrieval-Augmented Generation

When RAG context is enabled, StoryCraft retrieves relevant passages from your own manuscript before calling the IA model. The IA then generates with knowledge of your actual story — characters, plot developments, world details — rather than working blind from a single paragraph.

Two retrieval modes

  • Hybrid (recommended): Blends three signals — semantic cosine similarity via a 384-dimensional MiniLM-L6-v2 embedding model (~60%), lexical keyword overlap (~30%), and chapter recency (~10%). Works best for complex stories with rich character and world details.
  • Lexical only: Pure keyword matching — faster, works on any device without a local ML model, but misses semantic connections (e.g. won't retrieve a passage about "courage" when you query for "bravery").

Configuring RAG

  1. Go to Configurações → Advanced IA → Context retrieval mode and pick Hybrid or Lexical.
  2. Click Rebuild local search index to build or refresh the embedding index. Do this after importing a backup or after adding many new characters and world entries.
  3. Voltar in the IA Writing Studio, toggle Use RAG context in the Writer toolbar. The chunk badge (e.g. 4 chunks) shows how many passages were injected into the current request.

Where RAG is used

Continuar Writing, Brainstorm, Summarize, IA Critic, and the Quadro de Enredo Suggest next beat feature all use the same RAG pipeline when enabled.

Privacy: The index lives in your browser's OPFS. Only the final assembled prompt (not the raw index) travels to your cloud IA provider.
